title: What is the role of LoL in 3I
display_title: What is the role of LoL in 3I
wikitext:
[]: What is the role of LoL in 3I?             Quote Post by Tobuway25 » Sat Oct 09, 2010 12:42 am So it seems that Seele needed the LoL for their version of 3I. Witch seems odd because as I understand it the LoL was made to prevent things like 3I. Do the mass production Evas somehow counteract it? An explanation as to how 3I worked would be awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Oct 09, 2010 12:47 am I remember that this was one of the first epic discussions I got into about NGE not long after I came here, and I don't think we ever came up with any satisfactory answer. I do remember thinking that because the Lane basically fulfills the role of a giant phallic symbol, it seems to have something to do with turning 01 into a kind of soul womb/receptacle. I'm not sure how it works in the lolNGEmetascience world of the series, but it seems to be doing something along those lines.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Oct 09, 2010 1:05 am One of the oldest plot holes in the book, this one. The best answer is that Seele were planning to use it as some kind of restriction on Lilith's power, since it appears from their dialogue in EoE that the original plan was to kill all the Angels and then...reunify Rei with Lilith, perhaps, and use the Lance to force her into causing Instrumentality? Gendou therefore forces their hand by getting rid of the Lance, forcing them to come up with an alternate plan: the use of Unit 01. But it's not clear why Seele thought this was a good idea, since Unit 01 is at this point rather more powerful than Lilith - possessing an S2 organ and containing a soul demonstrably in opposition to Seele, I'd consider her rather more of a threat than an inert marshmallow woman who's still crucified. It's not until Gendou fuses Adam with Rei - something that was certainly never supposed to happen in Seele's scenario - that Lilith becomes a threat to the scenario (and in fact usurps it within seconds). And all that winds up being a little moot, since as soon as Unit 01 awakens she summons back the Lance by AT-field-wankery in order to fuse with it - and use it for what...? I don't know, the real problem with resolving this shit is that we don't even know what the intended scenarios of the various factions in EoE were, and what actually happens is pretty far from any of them and winds up being a colossal clusterfuck of motivations running into each other to the point where it's all I can do to tell who's in charge of the process at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Oct 09, 2010 2:08 pm Easy Answer: If it can restrict power, it can probably enhance it too.

[]: Re: What is the role of LoL in 3I?             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Oct 09, 2010 7:11 pm Tobuway25 wrote: So it seems that Seele needed the LoL for their version of 3I. Witch seems odd because as I understand it the LoL was made to prevent things like 3I. Do the mass production Evas somehow counteract it? An explanation as to how 3I worked would be awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Sat Oct 09, 2010 7:55 pm I have a different theory: The Lance of longinus is made of a similar material of the core, that is why when we see it fusing with unit 01, it fuses with the core and the entire Unit and tree of life appears to be the same material... As stated on the CI, the LoL is a "safe" mechanism, but also, as we can see in the 3i it can have a different fuction, of fully fuse the core with the body of the Eva/SoL and maybe, by this fusion, release all the mighty divine power of the s2 organ/core... One hint of this property of "fusing/uniting", is the fact that differently from when we see the lance into Lillith, or destroyng the orbit angel, of even in the extra pictures of the director's cut, of Adam found on antartica with the edges being shown, the only time we see in fact the lance being inserted with the united side, instead of the separated edges, is when it becomes the tree of life... So if used with the separated side, it has the function to disable/separate the core from it's host body, and when inserted on the opposite, fused side, it completes fuses, activates the core/s2 engine.... Personally i have the theory that the core is the source of the power of the evas and angels, even tough most ppl here isn´t aware of this theory or don´t agree you could also extrapolate this by saying it is applying to the S2 engine instead of just the core....

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Oct 09, 2010 8:39 pm Azathoth wrote: One of the oldest plot holes in the book, this one. The best answer is that Seele were planning to use it as some kind of restriction on Lilith's power, since it appears from their dialogue in EoE that the original plan was to kill all the Angels and then...reunify Rei with Lilith, perhaps, and use the Lance to force her into causing Instrumentality?

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Oct 09, 2010 8:40 pm Welcome back.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Oct 09, 2010 8:53 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I've often wondered if The Lance, being a phallic symbol, was itself the key to how souls entered into the Seeds.
